Thought 3: Since the murder weapon was the prop halberd from the play, Elizabeth has direct access to it as the director with keys to all rooms. Freya, while owning a halberd, would not have easy access to the prop weapon unless she stole it, which isn't indicated. Additionally, Elizabeth has a strong motive (affair and professional threat) and her "long showers" could be a cover for cleaning the weapon or being absent. Freya has a motive (debt and exposure) but also a public alibi ("so many witnessed" her leaving after the argument). The alibi seems to place Freya away from the scene, while Elizabeth has no alibi and opportunity.
